ddls:
CREATE TABLE "hardware_units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hardware unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the hardware unit */,
  "unit_type" TEXT /* Type of hardware unit (e.g., Sensor, Actuator) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the hardware unit */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the hardware unit */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status (e.g., online, offline, maintenance) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the hardware unit */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the hardware unit */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number for identification */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the hardware unit was installed */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "control_signals" (
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each control signal */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware unit associated with the signal */,
  "signal_name" TEXT /* Name of the control signal */,
  "signal_value" TEXT /* Current value of the control signal */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the signal */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum value the signal can take */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum value the signal can take */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the signal */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the signal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_control_signals_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hardware_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "read_operations" (
  "read_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each read operation */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware unit that was read */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the control signal that was read */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the read operation */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value read from the control signal */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the read operation */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the value read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("read_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_read_operations_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES control_signals ("signal_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_read_operations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_read_operations_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hardware_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "write_operations" (
  "write_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each write operation */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware unit that was written to */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the control signal that was written to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the write operation */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value written to the control signal */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the write operation */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the value written */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("write_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_write_operations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_write_operations_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hardware_units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_write_operations_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES control_signals ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "control_words" (
  "control_word_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each control word */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware unit associated with the control word */,
  "word_name" TEXT /* Name of the control word */,
  "word_value" TEXT /* Value of the control word */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the control word */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who configured the control word */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the control word value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("control_word_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_control_words_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_control_words_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hardware_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "counters" (
  "counter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each counter */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware unit associated with the counter */,
  "counter_name" TEXT /* Name of the counter */,
  "current_value" INTEGER /* Current value of the counter */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who last updated the counter */,
  "min_value" INTEGER /* Minimum value the counter can take */,
  "max_value" INTEGER /* Maximum value the counter can take */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("counter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_counters_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hardware_units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_counters_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_settings" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware unit associated with the setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the setting */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who last updated the setting */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the setting value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_settings_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hardware_units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_settings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, technician, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has admin privileges */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who accessed the system */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "operation" TEXT /* Operation performed by the user (e.g., read, write, config change) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client */,
  "success" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access attempt was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware unit associated with the metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., response time, error rate) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metric was recorded */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who recorded the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hardware_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware unit associated with the alert */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was generated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who generated or acknowledged the alert */,
  "acknowledged" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alert has been acknowledged */,
  "acknowledged_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was acknowledged */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hardware_units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was sent */,
  "read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read */,
  "read_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_health" (
  "health_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health check entry */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware unit being checked */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current health status of the unit (e.g., operational, failure) */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last health check */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the health check */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the health status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("health_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_health_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_health_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hardware_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the configuration was created */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who created the configuration */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the configuration is the default one */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_configurations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
